tasks.title: WAVE06 API: Publish the route-family migration matrix and touched public reason vocabulary
tasks.prompt: Use `lib/design/design-plan.json`, `../socialpredict/README/PRODUCTION-NOTES/BACKEND/06-api-design.md`, `../socialpredict/README/PRODUCTION-NOTES/BACKEND/FUTURE/02-long-term-api-design.md`, and `../socialpredict/backend/docs/API-ISSUES.md` as the binding design context for WAVE06 API alignment. Start the wave by making the mixed contract explicit instead of improvising it during code cleanup. Add an initial route-family migration matrix for the touched `/v0` families plus infra docs routes, classify which paths are envelope-based, mixed raw-success plus `ReasonResponse`, or legacy plain-text, capture the touched public `reason` vocabulary, and make every touched `PlainTextErrorResponse` usage explicit migration state. Keep `backend/server/server.go` as the source of truth and align `backend/docs/openapi.yaml`, route/spec checks, and API follow-up notes to that matrix without introducing a universal wrapper, `/v1`, header-based versioning, or generated-client workflow.
acceptance_criteria: The touched docs and OpenAPI contract include a route-family migration matrix that matches live route behavior for the chosen families and infra docs routes.

Touched public `reason` values are explicit and kept separate from runtime or telemetry vocabulary, and touched `PlainTextErrorResponse` usage is documented as migration state rather than target contract.
Focused contract validation keeps the matrix slice aligned with `server.go`, touched handlers or DTOs, and `openapi.yaml`.

focus: OpenAPI parity for the touched families and infra docs routes

notes: Track middleware and handler response families together in the matrix.
Keep the source-of-truth order explicit: `server.go` -> touched handlers or DTOs -> `openapi.yaml` -> `API-ISSUES.md`.

tasks.title: WAVE06 API: Introduce boundary-safe auth outcomes for the first protected route family
tasks.prompt: With the migration matrix in place, start retiring auth transport leakage in code rather than just documenting it. Replace `internal/service/auth.HTTPError` usage on one coherent protected route family, such as the current-user/profile, admin, or create-market slice, with typed internal outcomes or an equivalent boundary-safe translation seam that keeps HTTP mapping at the handler or boundary helper. Preserve the existing `POST /v0/login` token response and the intentional `/v0/changepassword` exception when `mustChangePassword` is set, keep public `reason` values separate from telemetry classification, and update OpenAPI or notes only for the touched routes instead of claiming wave-wide completion.
acceptance_criteria: The chosen auth path no longer requires `internal/service/auth.HTTPError` as the internal contract for the touched protected route family, and HTTP mapping stays at the boundary.
The touched protected routes preserve the existing login token contract plus the intentional login and change-password `mustChangePassword` exception behavior.
Focused route tests and OpenAPI or note updates cover the migrated auth failure reasons without leaking raw transport strings or overstating broader retirement.

focus: boundary-safe auth outcomes for the chosen route family
removal of `HTTPError` transport leakage from the touched auth path

out_of_scope: token redesign, MFA, RBAC, or session-platform work
full authenticated-route sweep in one task
turning public `reason` values into telemetry fields
notes: Choose one coherent protected family instead of scattering single-callsite changes across the tree.
Preserve the existing login and change-password exception behavior intentionally while retiring transport leakage in the touched slice.

tasks.title: WAVE06 API: Converge the first client-visible legacy route family on shared failure writers
tasks.prompt: After the first auth-translation seam lands, migrate one client-visible legacy route family that still returns raw JSON success with plain-text or ad hoc `http.Error` failures, such as search markets or a public-user reporting slice, onto the shared failure-writer direction. Keep success payloads truthful to the current family unless the route already advertises an envelope, update the route-family migration matrix and OpenAPI route-by-route, and only retire `PlainTextErrorResponse` for the paths whose handler and middleware behavior have actually converged. Do not attempt a flag-day public route redesign or a universal response wrapper.
acceptance_criteria: The chosen legacy route family no longer emits touched ad hoc plain-text failures where the shared boundary-failure path now applies.
Success payloads for the chosen family remain stable and truthful to the current route contract instead of being force-wrapped.
Focused tests, the migration matrix, and OpenAPI updates reflect the migrated family and make remaining `PlainTextErrorResponse` families explicit elsewhere.

focus: shared failure-writer behavior for the migrated family
stable success-shape preservation without wrapper drift

notes: Prefer a route family with existing contract tests or clear client visibility.
Migrate middleware and handler behavior together for the chosen family.

tasks.title: WAVE06 API: Make Swagger and OpenAPI publishing explicit across proxy environments
tasks.prompt: Once the active contract inventory is explicit, make backend-served docs an owned publishing contract across local, staging, and production. Preserve `/swagger/` and `/openapi.yaml` as the canonical backend endpoints, update the repo's proxy or deployment templates and operator-facing notes so those paths are published deliberately instead of being hidden behind `/api/` prefix routing, and keep the backend as the single Swagger or OpenAPI source of truth. Do not solve this by cloning Swagger into the frontend, inventing a new docs platform, or starting generated-client rollout.
acceptance_criteria: The chosen staging or production publishing path for `/swagger/` and `/openapi.yaml` is explicit in the touched proxy or deployment config and operator docs rather than implicit prefix routing.
Backend tests and touched docs agree on the canonical backend docs endpoints and their intended external publishing behavior.
The task keeps backend-served docs as the single contract surface and does not introduce a frontend-owned Swagger copy or new platform layer.

focus: agreement between backend tests and operator-facing docs
continued backend ownership of the docs surface

out_of_scope: generated clients or generator-first contract ownership
notes: Choose one explicit external docs path or hostname and document any access-restriction decision.
Do not let `/api/` proxy rules accidentally own the docs contract.

tasks.title: WAVE06 API: Stop and review remaining auth leakage, PlainTextErrorResponse families, and deferred platform ideas
tasks.prompt: Finish WAVE06 with an API-specific stop-and-review point. Remove or document any touched remaining auth transport leakage or plain-text boundary failures that still bypass the current shared contract, refresh the route-family migration matrix with what this wave actually changed, and leave a precise next seam for the remaining `PlainTextErrorResponse` families or auth call sites. Keep `FUTURE/02-long-term-api-design.md` explicitly deferred, and do not pull HATEOAS, XML content negotiation, universal `APIResponse` wrappers, header-based versioning, `/v1`, generated-client-first workflow, or an API service or platform layer back into the active queue.
acceptance_criteria: The remaining touched auth transport leaks and remaining `PlainTextErrorResponse` route families are either removed or explicitly inventoried with a precise next migration seam.
`API-ISSUES.md` and the active API notes accurately distinguish WAVE06 outcomes, remaining migration state, and the chosen docs-publishing contract.
The wave ends with a concrete next API or auth slice rather than a revived platform backlog.

focus: active-note accuracy versus touched code and proxy config
continued deferral of long-term API-platform topics

out_of_scope: greenfield route redesign or universal wrapper rollout
notes: This is the WAVE06 stop-and-review point before any broader API platform or versioning discussion.
Keep the next slice framed as a concrete route family or auth seam.
